subject: general
title: How to assess new Class 3 students' level on the very first day
query_text: How to assess new Class 3 students' level on the very first day

immediate_steps.title: Immediate Action
steps: Greet students warmly and create a relaxed, non-threatening atmosphere — make clear this is not a test, just a friendly conversation
Use a simple 3-level oral check: ask students to tell you their name, count to 10, and read 2–3 words from the blackboard
Quietly note which students respond confidently, which hesitate, and which stay silent — this gives you three rough groups without any formal paperwork

classroom_activity.title: Classroom Activity — 'Picture Talk' Baseline
classroom_activity.activity: Draw a simple scene on the blackboard (a house, tree, sun, child). Ask students to: (1) name what they see, (2) say one sentence about the picture, (3) write one word they see. Observe who can do all three, who can do one or two, and who needs support. This simultaneously checks listening, speaking, and early writing — the three foundational skills.
classroom_activity.materials: Blackboard, chalk, students' notebooks or loose paper

inclusion_strategy.title: Inclusion Strategy
inclusion_strategy.strategy: For students who stay silent or cannot write, sit beside them briefly and ask them to point to objects in the picture or repeat a word after you. This gives you a baseline for pre-literacy learners without making them feel singled out in front of peers.

reflection_prompt.title: Reflection
reflection_prompt.question: After today, could you identify at least two students who may need foundational support — and two who are ready for grade-level work?

source_citation.title: Source
source_citation.reference: NIPUN Bharat Framework (2021) — Foundational Literacy & Numeracy baseline assessment principles; NCERT Class 3 Santoor — self-assessment and oral language guidelines (pp. 67, 128)
